title: "Reading companion"
---

# <img src="../assets/hub-icons/book.svg" class="hub-inline-icon" alt="" aria-hidden="true" /> Reading companion

These chapters follow the same story as the lecture decks, but at a slower pace. Use them before class, after class, or when you miss a session and need the ideas explained in plain language.

We keep two example tracks across the week: **simulated genes** (where we know the ground truth) and **[Palmer Penguins](../data/cards/palmer-penguins.qmd)** (real measurements that are messier and more realistic). For context on every dataset, use the **[data catalog](../data/index.qmd)**.

If you want the overall narrative first, read the **[curriculum outline](../curriculum-outline.qmd)**. If you want the long coding versions, jump to the **[student notebooks](../notebooks/index.qmd)**.

| Chapter | Teaching day | What you will understand | Open |
|--------|--------------|--------------------------|------|
| [Chapter 1 — Learning from measurements](#chapter-01-learning-from-measurements) | Day 1 (Mon) | Why train/validation/test splits matter and how to avoid leakage | [Open](module-01-foundations.qmd) |
| [Chapter 2 — When coefficients need restraint](#chapter-02-when-coefficients-need-restraint) | Day 1 (Mon) | Ridge, lasso, and elastic net in plain language | [Open](module-02-regularization.qmd) |
| [Chapter 3 — Rules and trees](#chapter-03-rules-and-trees) | Day 2 (Tue) | How tree models split data and why “two cultures” helps | [Open](module-03-trees-and-cultures.qmd) |
| [Chapter 4 — A reproducible modeling workflow](#chapter-04-a-reproducible-modeling-workflow) | Day 2 (Tue) | How `recipe`, `workflow`, `last_fit`, and tuning fit together | [Open](module-04-pipeline.qmd) |
| [Chapter 5 — Stronger learners, same discipline](#chapter-05-stronger-learners-same-discipline) | Day 4 (Thu) | How random forests, boosting, and MLPs fit into the same pipeline | [Open](module-05-advanced-models.qmd) |
| [Chapter 6 — Scores that match the question](#chapter-06-scores-that-match-the-question) | Day 4 (Thu) | How to choose metrics under imbalance and read SHAP cautiously | [Open](module-06-evaluation-and-interpretability.qmd) |
| [Chapter 7 — Choosing what to optimize](#chapter-07-choosing-what-to-optimize) | Day 4 / lab | Why different tuning metrics pick different “best” settings | [Open](module-07-penguins-choose-metrics.qmd) |
| [Chapter 8 — Comparing learners fairly](#chapter-08-comparing-learners-fairly) | Day 4 / lab | How to compare model families fairly with fixed folds and recipe | [Open](module-08-penguins-compare-models.qmd) |

---

## Chapter 1 — Learning from measurements {#chapter-01-learning-from-measurements}

This chapter sets up the full course: prediction as a disciplined process, not just fitting a line. You will see the same logic on simulated genes and penguins.

**[Open Chapter 1 →](module-01-foundations.qmd)**

---

## Chapter 2 — When coefficients need restraint {#chapter-02-when-coefficients-need-restraint}

This chapter explains why regularization helps when predictors are correlated or numerous, and how ridge, lasso, and elastic net differ in practice.

**[Open Chapter 2 →](module-02-regularization.qmd)**

---

## Chapter 3 — Rules and trees {#chapter-03-rules-and-trees}

This chapter introduces decision trees as nested if-then rules and places them in the broader “two cultures” view: assumptions-first versus prediction-first.

**[Open Chapter 3 →](module-03-trees-and-cultures.qmd)**

---

## Chapter 4 — A reproducible modeling workflow {#chapter-04-a-reproducible-modeling-workflow}

This chapter turns modeling into a repeatable workflow: one honest train/test example first, then the same tuned tree pipeline used in Day 2 slides.

**[Open Chapter 4 →](module-04-pipeline.qmd)**

---

## Chapter 5 — Stronger learners, same discipline {#chapter-05-stronger-learners-same-discipline}

This chapter explains what changes and what stays fixed when you move from single trees to random forests, boosting, and small neural nets.

**[Open Chapter 5 →](module-05-advanced-models.qmd)**

---

## Chapter 6 — Scores that match the question {#chapter-06-scores-that-match-the-question}

This chapter focuses on decision-aware evaluation: choosing metrics for the question at hand, handling class imbalance, and reading SHAP with causal caution.

**[Open Chapter 6 →](module-06-evaluation-and-interpretability.qmd)**

---

## Chapter 7 — Choosing what to optimize {#chapter-07-choosing-what-to-optimize}

This extension chapter shows how metric choice changes tuning outcomes, even when you keep data, folds, and model family fixed.

**[Open Chapter 7 →](module-07-penguins-choose-metrics.qmd)**

---

## Chapter 8 — Comparing learners fairly {#chapter-08-comparing-learners-fairly}

This extension chapter compares three stronger learners fairly by holding preprocessing and folds fixed and changing only the model engine.

**[Open Chapter 8 →](module-08-penguins-compare-models.qmd)**
